name: Exothermic reaction sign of ΔH
question_text: In an exothermic reaction, what is the sign of the enthalpy change (ΔH)?
general_feedback: Exothermic reactions release heat to the surroundings, so the system's enthalpy decreases. By convention ΔH = Hproducts − Hreactants is negative.
correct_feedback: Correct — Heat is released, so the system loses enthalpy; ΔH < 0.

answers.feedback: Heat is released, so the system loses enthalpy; ΔH < 0.
Positive ΔH means the system absorbs heat — that is endothermic, the opposite case.
Zero ΔH would mean no net heat exchange; exothermic reactions release heat.
ΔH is well-defined for any reaction with specified states; exothermic means ΔH < 0.
